messages.content: which word goes with the definition "people of a certain region who elect a representative who is then answerable to them" A. program B. administration C. representative democracy D. constituency E. partisan F. convention
D. constituency

which word goes with the definition “a type of person who will staunchly campaign or vote for only one political party” A. program B. administration C. representative democracy D. constituency E. partisan F. convention
E. partisan
which word goes with the definition “a political plan of action” A. program B. administration C. representative democracy D. constituency E. partisan F. convention
A. program

which word goes with the definition “the executive branch of the american government; particularly used in the relation to the presidency” A. program B. administration C. representative democracy D. constituency E. partisan F. convention
B. administration

which word goes with the definition “a political partys assembly, usually for the purpose of presenting a candidate” A. program B. administration C. representative democracy D. constituency E. partisan F. convention
F. convention
a political party is considered to practice _ if it puts it own agenda ahead of the good of the people A. republicanism B. third party mentality C. partisan politics
C. partisan politics
which of the following are democractic presidents? select all that apply A. kennedy B. bush C. nixon D. jackson E. carter

A. Kennedy
E. Carter
what are the major american political parties? select all that apply A. democractic b. republican C. labour D. consernative

A. Democratic
B. Republican

which words goes with " gave the president power to deport any non-citizen the government deemed dangerous and arrest critics of the government" A. allen and sedition act B. loose construction C. anti-federalist party D. strict construction E. central government
A. Alien and Sedition Act

which words goes with " limit the federal government strictly to the powers which the constitution specifically delegated to it" A. allen and sedition act B. loose construction C. anti-federalist party D. strict construction E. central government
D. strict construction

which words goes with " allowing the federal government powers not denied it within the constitution" A. allen and sedition act B. loose construction C. anti-federalist party D. strict construction E. central government
B. loose construction
which words goes with "the federal power of the country" A. allen and sedition act B. loose construction C. anti-federalist party D. strict construction E. central government
E. central government
the anti federalist would limit the federal government strictly to A. managing of foreign affairs B. coining of money C. the powers the constitution delegates to it
C. the powers the constitution delegates to it
the anti-federalists believed in a _ interpretation of the constitution A. flexible B. loose C. strict
C. strict
the _ believed that the federal government should possess all powers not specifically denied to it A. federalists B. anti-federalists
A. federalists
the _ believed in a loose construction of the constitution A. antifederalists B. federalists
B. federalists
the only democrats to serve as president from 1890 to 1916 were _ select all that apply A. wilson B. douglas C. cleveland D. lincoln

A. Wilson
C. Cleveland

what is one reason the democrats held power for so long in the 30s 40s and 50s A. rampant inflation due to securities fraud B. the republicans got blamed for the great depression C. the house and senate agreed to admit women to congress D. the democrats supported voting rights for indigenous americans
B. the republicans got blamed for the great depression

select ALL of the true statements about the democratic party A. it is the oldest political party in the US B. abraham lincoln was a democrat C. the democratic party won considerable political strength during the civil war D. the democrats won the presidency in the 90's but lost control of congress E. in the 1950s democrats fought over the institution of slavery in california

A. it is the oldest political party in the US
C. the Democratic Party won considerable political strength during the civil war
D. the Democrats won the presidency in the 90's but lost control of congress

the democratic split was so severe during the 1860 elections that A. the party disbanded B. they ran two candidates for the presidency C. the majority of the memebers joined the republican party D. they had no candidates running for the presidential election
B. they ran two candidates for the presidency
the democrats won all the elections from 1828-1860 EXCERPT IN A. 1829 and 1855 B. 1840 and 1848 C. 1836 and 1842
the democrats fought bitterly at times over A. banking policies B. slavery issues C. tariff rates D. all of the above
D. all of the above
